verse_specific_anchors: iha (here, on this path of buddhi-yoga just opened at 2.39)
bahu-śākhā ... avyavasāyinām (many-branched of the irresolute — the proliferation as diagnostic)
verdict_reason: The engineering analog hangs specifically on the contrast 'one resolved buddhi' vs 'many-branched intelligences of the irresolute,' which is the exact structural claim of 2.41. Could the analog come from another verse? Not 2.47 (which is about action vs fruits), not 2.42-44 (which is about the kāmya-fixation pathology with a different cause), not 2.48 (which is about samatvam as state). The 'branching as diagnostic of irresolution' framing requires precisely 2.41's contrast structure — bahu-śākhā vs ekā buddhi — and the falsifiability/counter-example correctly distinguish it from 'just commit' (2.31-style duty injunctions) and 'detach from outcome' (2.47). PASS for both 7.6 (cross-verse force-fit) and 3.5 (force-fit test).
